data.title: Travelers Is Maintained at Overweight by Piper Sandler
State Street's Earnings Beat Expectations. Why the Stock Is Falling Anyway. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Nate Wolf

Shares of State Street sank Friday even after the bank and asset- management company reported better-than-expected quarterly earnings.

State Street posted earnings of $2.78 a share for the third quarter, surpassing analysts' consensus estimate of $2.64. Revenue totaled $3.55 billion, up around 9% from the year prior and ahead of Wall Street's call for $3.46 billion.

Investors may have been disappointed by a drop in net interest income, which fell 2% from last quarter and 1% from last year. Analysts polled by FactSet had expected interest income to rise slightly in the quarter.

State Street blamed the decline on lower average short-term interest rates and changes in the mix of deposits.

The stock was down 4.8% to $107.50 on Friday, putting it on pace for its lowest close since July 17, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

Shares have climbed around 10% this year, but Friday marks a second consecutive decline following earnings. State Street stock fell in July after earnings fell short of expectations.

Write to Nate Wolf at nate.wolf@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: India's IndiGo Orders 30 Additional A350-900 Airbus Aircraft
data.description:
By Cristina Gallardo

Airbus said IndiGo, India's largest airline, placed firm order for 30 additional A350-900 aircraft, as part of its plan to enter the long-haul international market.

These new 30 aircraft will expand IndiGo's total A350 orders to 60 planes, Airbus said Friday.

The two companies had signed a memorandum of understanding in June agreeing to this order, the aerospace group added.

Write to Cristina Gallardo at cristina.gallardo@wsj.com

data.title: Avita Medical Cut to Hold From Buy by Lake Street
Argentina stocks outperform in LatAm after US support lifts confidence
data.description:
Key points:
  • Argentina's Merval index rises on Bessent support
  • Regional equities slip, capping volatile week
  • UBS upgrades EM equities

By Niket Nishant

Argentina's stocks gained 1.1% on Friday after another nod of confidence from Washington, outperforming as Latin American markets limped towards the end of a volatile week.

U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent reiterated support for Argentina, adding to a string of measures this week that have eased fears after President Donald Trump hinted that aid might hinge on President Javier Milei's party doing well in midterm elections.

"The long-term picture is one of increasing cooperation between the two countries. The number one focus is to move past the headlines of the midterm elections," said Thea Jamison, founder of investment management firm CHANGE Global.

The Milei administration is seeking to restore investor confidence amid persistent inflation, fiscal strain, and a fragile political landscape.

Brazil's benchmark index rose 0.5%, helped by optimism around upcoming corporate earnings despite pressure from expiring stock options and weak cues from U.S. markets. Option expirations often trigger portfolio adjustments and volatility, as traders unwind positions and rebalance.

U.S. and Brazilian officials held trade talks on Thursday and agreed to work to schedule a meeting between Trump and his counterpart Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva.

Citi analysts cautioned that even if tariff risks ease, uncertainty related to the domestic political environment could weigh on sentiment.

Still, some companies could benefit from the government's plans to offer financial guarantees to fund strategic mineral projects and tax incentives for their processing and industrialization.

An index tracking Latin American equities (.MILA00000PUS) and a parallel gauge of regional currencies (.MILA00000CUS) each slipped 0.3% but remained on track to end the week up 2.2% and 0.6%, respectively.

Colombia's stock market dipped 0.2% but was on course for its second consecutive week of gains. The peso strengthened against the dollar.

Raising the country's key interest rate for the first time in over two years is "on the table" in the face of several risks that could speed up inflation, Colombian central bank board member Mauricio Villamizar said in an interview with Bloomberg News published on Friday.

The remarks came days after Colombia's Senate gave the final approval to the government's 2026 budget, sending it to the president to sign.

Mexican stocks retreated 1.2% after two sessions of gains.

Elsewhere, pockets of global assets found support as easing tensions in the Middle East helped remove a long-standing drag on risk sentiment. Long-dated Israeli bonds gained.

Brokerage UBS Global also upgraded emerging market equities to "attractive".

data.title: Week Ahead - Oct 20th
data.description:
Next week, investors will continue to monitor developments in the US–China trade dispute, watching for signs of either escalation or easing tensions.

The US corporate earnings season will also move into full swing, with major companies such as Tesla, P&G, GE, Coca-Cola, Netflix, IBM, AT&T, Verizon, and Intel set to report results.

In the US, the federal government shutdown is expected to enter its fourth week, though traders will still have the CPI report to digest, alongside S&P Global flash PMIs, and existing home sales.

In China, attention will focus on a batch of crucial economic releases, including Q3 GDP. Elsewhere, flash PMI data will be released for the Eurozone, Germany, the UK, India, Japan, and Australia.

Other important indicators include Euro Area consumer confidence, Japan’s trade and inflation figures, UK inflation, as well as monetary policy decisions from the central banks of Turkey, Indonesia, and South Korea.

data.title: Indigo to convert 30 of its 70 purchase rights for Airbus A350-900 jets into firm orders
data.description: India's budget airline IndiGo .NS> said on Friday it has signed a contract with Airbus to convert 30 of its 70 purchase rights for A350-900 jets into firm orders.

data.description:
RNS Number : 8999D HSS Hire Group PLC 17 October 2025  

Please note that from 22 March 2021, the Standard TR-1 Form should be completed and submitted to the FCA via our Electronic Submission System (ESS) in relation to notifications of voting rights held in issuers whose shares are admitted to trading on UK regulated markets.

Holders of voting rights in issuers whose shares are admitted to trading on UK prescribed markets such as AIM market, can continue to use this form to send their notifications to those issuers. Alternatively, if they wish they can register on ESS to be able to notify to us, produce a TR-1 Form via ESS and submit the downloaded version to issuers with shares admitted to trading on  prescribed markets.

More information on how to submit a TR-1 Form via ESS is available here

Name of the proxy holder

The number and % of voting rights held

The date until which the voting rights will be held

11. Additional information xvi

Place of completion

Guernsey

Date of completion

17/10/2025

Notes

i Please note this form should be read jointly with the applicable Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules Chapter 5 (DTR5) available on the following link: https://www.handbook.fca.org.uk/handbook/DTR/5/?view=chapter

ii Full name of the legal entity and further specification of the issuer or underlying issuer, provided it is reliable and accurate (e.g. address, LEI, domestic number identity). Indicate in the relevant section whether the issuer is a non UK issuer.

iii Other reason for the notification could be voluntary notifications, changes of attribution of the nature of the holding (e.g. expiring of financial instruments) or acting in concert.

iv This should be the full name of (a) the shareholder; (b) the natural person or legal entity acquiring, disposing of or exercising voting rights in the cases provided for in DTR5.2.1 (b) to (h); (c) all parties to the agreement referred to in DTR5.2.1 (a) or (d) the holder of financial instruments referred to in DTR5.3.1.

As the disclosure of cases of acting in concert may vary due to the specific circumstances (e.g. same or different total positions of the parties, entering or exiting of acting in concert by a single party) the standard form does not provide for a specific method how to notify cases of acting in concert.

In relation to the transactions referred to in  DTR5.2.1 (b) to (h), the following list is provided as indication of the persons who should be mentioned:

- in the circumstances foreseen in DTR5.2.1 (b), the natural person or legal entity that acquires the voting rights and is entitled to exercise them under the agreement and the natural person or legal entity who is transferring temporarily for consideration the voting rights;

- in the circumstances foreseen in DTR5.2.1 (c), the natural person or legal entity holding the collateral, provided the person or entity controls the voting rights and declares its intention of exercising them, and natural person or legal entity lodging the collateral under these conditions;

- in the circumstances foreseen in DTR5.2.1 (d), the natural person or legal entity who has a life interest in shares if that person or entity is entitled to exercise the voting rights attached to the shares and the natural person or legal entity who is disposing of the voting rights when the life interest is created;

- in the circumstances foreseen in DTR5.2.1 (e), the controlling natural person or legal entity and, provided it has a notification duty at an individual level under DTR 5.1, under DTR5.2.1 (a) to (d) or under a combination of any of those situations, the controlled undertaking;

- in the circumstances foreseen in DTR5.2.1 (f), the deposit taker of the shares, if he can exercise the voting rights attached to the shares deposited with him at his discretion, and the depositor of the shares allowing the deposit taker to exercise the voting rights at his discretion;

- in the circumstances foreseen in DTR5.2.1 (g), the natural person or legal entity that controls the voting rights;

- in the circumstances foreseen in DTR5.2.1 (h), the proxy holder, if he can exercise the voting rights at his discretion, and the shareholder who has given his proxy to the proxy holder allowing the latter to exercise the voting rights at his discretion (e.g. management companies).

v Applicable in the cases provided for in DTR5.2.1 (b) to (h). This should be the full name of the shareholder who is the counterparty to the natural person or legal entity referred to DTR5.2 unless the percentage of voting rights held by the shareholder is lower than the lowest notifiable threshold for the disclosure of voting rights holdings in accordance with national practices (e.g. identification of funds managed by management companies).

vi The date on which threshold is crossed or reached should be the date on which the acquisition or disposal took place or the other reason triggered the notification obligation. For passive crossings, the date when the corporate event took effect.

vii The total number of voting rights held in the issuer shall be composed of all the shares, including depository receipts representing shares, to which voting rights are attached even if the exercise thereof is suspended.

viii If the holding has fallen below the lowest applicable threshold, please note that it might not be necessary to disclose the extent of the holding, only that the new holding is below that threshold.

ix In case of combined holdings of shares with voting rights attached "direct holding" and voting rights "indirect holding", please split the voting rights number and percentage into the direct and indirect columns - if there is no combined holdings, please leave the relevant box blank.

x Date of maturity/expiration of the financial instrument i.e. the date when right to acquire shares ends.

xi If the financial instrument has such a period - please specify this period - for example once every 3 months starting from [date].

xii In case of cash settled instruments the number and percentages of voting rights is to be presented on a delta-adjusted basis (DTR 5.3.3.A).

xiii If the person subject to the notification obligation is either controlled and/or does control another undertaking then the second option applies.

xiv The full chain of controlled undertakings starting with the ultimate controlling natural person or legal entity has to be presented also in the cases, in which only on subsidiary level a threshold is crossed or reached and the subsidiary undertaking discloses the notification as only in this way will the markets get always the full picture of the group holdings. In case of multiple chains through which the voting rights and/or financial instruments are effectively held the chains have to be presented chain by chain by numbering each chain accordingly. Please see the below example: 

Name of ultimate controlling person A (chain 1)

Name of controlled undertaking B

Name of controlled undertaking C

Name of ultimate controlling person A (chain 2)

Name of controlled undertaking B

Name of controlled undertaking D

Name of ultimate controlling person A (chain3)

Name of controlled undertaking E

Name of controlled undertaking F

xv The names of controlled undertakings through which the voting rights and/or financial instruments are effectively held have to be presented irrespectively whether the controlled undertakings cross or reach the lowest applicable threshold themselves.

xvi Example: Correction of a previous notification.

This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact rns@lseg.com or visit www.rns.com.

RNS may use your IP address to confirm compliance with the terms and conditions, to analyse how you engage with the information contained in this communication, and to share such analysis on an anonymised basis with others as part of our commercial services. For further information about how RNS and the London Stock Exchange use the personal data you provide us, please see our Privacy Policy.  END  HOLEASEXFFXSFFA

data.title: Italy to raise flat tax on super rich to 300,000 euros, officials say
data.description:
Italy plans to raise by 50% the rate of a flat tax designed to attract wealthy individuals, as part of measures to fund its 2026-2028 budget plan, two officials close to the matter told Reuters.

The government intends to increase the annual levy to 300,000 euros from 200,000 euros on foreign-sourced income earned by individuals who transfer their tax residence to Italy, the officials said.

The move, included in the draft 2026 budget law, marks the second hike in two years.

First introduced in 2017 by Prime Minister Matteo Renzi's centre-left government to attract wealthy foreigners, the levy was doubled to 200,000 in 2024 by Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni's conservative administration, with no retroactive effect.

Nearly 1,500 individuals took advantage of Italy's flat tax regime in 2023, according to June data from the Court of Auditors. The measure generated 315 million euros in revenue for the state between 2020 and 2023.

Portuguese soccer star Cristiano Ronaldo, who played for Italy's Serie A club Juventus between 2018 and 2021, was among the high‑profile beneficiaries of Italy's flat tax regime.

($1 = 0.8548 euros)

data.title: REG - Cropper(James) PLC - PDMR and PCA Shareholdings
data.description:
RNS Number : 8994D Cropper(James) PLC 17 October 2025  

James Cropper plc

(the "Company")

PDMR and PCA Shareholdings

The Company has been notified that, on 17 October 2025, Mark Cropper (Non-Executive Chair) transferred a total of 147,384 ordinary shares of 25p each ("Shares") in the Company to his sons, James Cropper and Jemima Cropper.

Of the total 147,384 Shares transferred:

·

71,192 Shares were transferred to James Cropper, with 50,919 Shares being transferred at a price of £3.18 per Share and 20,273 Shares transferred for nil consideration; and

·

76,192 Shares were transferred to Jemima Cropper, with 54,025 Shares being transferred at a price of £3.18 per Share and 22,167 transferred for nil consideration.

In addition, following a change in age, the Company has been notified that 26,349 Shares in the Company previously reported within Mark Cropper's beneficial ownership have now transferred to the beneficial ownership of his son, Jemima Cropper for nil consideration.

Following the above changes, the beneficial interests of Mark Cropper, James Cropper and Jemima Cropper in the Company's share capital are as follows:

data.title: Brazil's Prio rises after resuming production at Peregrino FPSO
data.description:
** Shares in Brazilian oil firm Prio rise after it said the Peregrino FPSO was authorized to resume production

** Peregrino is operated by Norway's Equinor , but Prio is set to become its operator after agreeing earlier this year to buy Equinor's stake

** Regulator ANP had ordered a production halt in August citing the need for improvement in risk management documentation and the unit's deluge system

** Prio, which is a partner in the Peregrino consortium, says the production ramp-up process at the unit will begin immediately

** Analysts at Santander say they are "pleased with this development", adding they now expect Peregrino's production to ramp up to 100,000 bpd within four to five days

** Prio's Sao Paulo-listed shares rise more than 4.5% in midday trading, surging as the top gainer in the benchmark index Bovespa , which is up 0.4%

** Year-to-date, Prio is down more than 10%

data.title: Is The Kraft Heinz Company a Value Trap or Dividend Gem?
data.description:
Kraft Heinz Business Empire: From Ketchup to Kraft Mac & Cheese

The Kraft Heinz (NASDAQ: KHC) is a leading global food and beverage company formed in 2015 through the merger of Kraft Foods Group and H.J. Heinz. The company owns 200+ iconic brands (Heinz ketchup, Kraft cheese and Mac & Cheese, Oscar Mayer meats, Philadelphia cream cheese, Jell-O, Velveeta, Kool-Aid, etc.) and operates worldwide. In 2023, KHC generated roughly $27 billion in net sales. Headquartered in Chicago and Pittsburgh, its business spans condiments, sauces, cheese, meats, snacks, and beverages, serving retail, foodservice, and away-from-home channels. Berkshire Hathaway (Warren Buffett (Trades, Portfolio)) and 3G Capital spearheaded the merger, with Berkshire currently the largest shareholder. (Buffett himself has admitted that the KraftHeinz merger underperformed, saying he overpaid for Kraft.) As of mid-2025, Kraft Heinz was the #5 global food/beverage company by sales. Its broad portfolio and strong brands have historically generated stable cash flow, but recent years have seen margin pressures and stagnant volumes across the industry.

Q2 2025 Earnings: Revenue Decline but EPS Beats Wall Street Estimates

In Q2 2025, Kraft Heinz reported net sales of $6.35 billion, a 1.9% decline year-over-year. Organic sales (excluding FX and portfolio changes) were down ~2%.The company posted a GAAP operating loss of $8.0 billion (due to a one-time $9.3 billion non-cash impairment charge), but on an adjusted basis, operating income was $1.3 billion (down 7.5% YoY). Adjusted EPS was $0.69, beating the analyst consensus of $0.64, though this was 11.5% lower YoY. Gross profit margins compressed: GAAP gross margin fell to ~34.4% (100 basis points below the prior year). The GAAP net loss was $7.82 billion (mainly from the write-down), but adjusted net income was $1.57 billion (down from $1.79B prior), implying an adjusted net margin around 25%. Free cash flow was robust, about $1.50 billion in the quarter (up 28.5% YoY), reflecting working capital improvements and cost controls.

Kraft Heinz Stock Reaction: Flat Trading Despite Earnings Beat

KHC stock currently trades around $27 (early Sep 2025), near its 52-week low of ~$25.44 and well below its prior ~ $36 high. Year-to-date, the share price is down significantly (by roughly 25%) as the market digests slowing growth and strategic changes. Since the Q2 report in late July, KHC has been roughly flat (+0.7%), lagging the broader market. Its forward dividend yield is about 5.8% (one of the highest in the consumer staples sector), which has helped stabilize demand among income-oriented investors. In summary, KHC's recent performance is middling, with earnings slightly above expectations, but persistent sales/margin declines leave investors cautious.

Short-Term Outlook: Inflation, Volumes, and Dividend Stability in Focus

Looking ahead to the remainder of 2025, Kraft Heinz has reaffirmed its full-year guidance and remains confident in its ability to navigate a complex consumer environment. The company expects organic net sales to decline between 1.5% and 3.5% for the full year, reflecting softer volumes in certain categories but also continued strength in core global brands. Management reiterated its commitment to disciplined pricing and promotional strategies designed to balance competitiveness with profitability.

On the earnings side, Kraft Heinz projects adjusted EPS in the range of $2.51 to $2.67 for the year, supported by cost efficiencies, supply chain improvements, and productivity initiatives across the portfolio. Importantly, the company continues to target a free cash flow conversion rate above 90%, underscoring its strong cash generation profile. This provides ample financial flexibility to fund the dividend, reduce debt, and invest in brand support initiatives.

Management also highlighted its focus on brand-building investments, innovation pipelines, and operational execution. Increased marketing spend in the first half of the year has already contributed to stronger brand equity rankings, and similar investments will continue to drive consumer engagement. Despite volume pressures in some North American grocery categories, Kraft Heinz remains confident that its combination of strong brands, operational discipline, and resilient free cash flow will allow it to deliver on its 2025 objectives.

Strategic Shake-Up: Kraft Heinz's Spin-Off Into Two Companies

In September 2025, Kraft Heinz unveiled its boldest restructuring move since the landmark 2015 merger between Kraft Foods Group and H.J. Heinz. The company announced plans to split into two independent, publicly traded entities by late 2026, a decision management says will simplify operations, sharpen strategic focus, and ultimately unlock shareholder value. The breakup is designed to separate Kraft Heinz's faster-growing, globally oriented businesses from its slower-moving, largely North American staples.

The first company, to be called Global Taste Elevation Co., will house the brands with stronger international resonance and higher growth potential. This unit includes Heinz ketchup and sauces, Philadelphia cream cheese, Kraft Mac & Cheese, and Classico sauces, among others. In 2024, these businesses generated approximately $15.4 billion in sales, supported by a broad geographic reach across Europe, Asia, and Latin America. Its product portfolio skews toward categories like condiments and sauces, which are growing faster than traditional packaged grocery, and the company historically enjoys operating margins in the mid- to high-20s.

The second company, provisionally named North American Grocery Co., will primarily consist of U.S. and Canadian staples, including Oscar Mayer meats, Lunchables, Kraft Singles, Kool-Aid, Jell-O, and Maxwell House coffee. In 2024, this business generated about $10.4 billion in sales, but its categories tend to be mature or in outright decline, particularly cold cuts and powdered beverages. Operating margins here are thinner, typically in the mid-teens, reflecting lower growth and higher competitive pressures from private labelspp.

Management has confirmed that both entities will retain investment-grade credit ratings and target leverage around 2.5x net debt-to-EBITDA. Importantly, Kraft Heinz committed to sustaining the combined dividend, which currently stands at $1.60 per share annuallya yield of about 5.8% at current prices. Post spin-off, the dividend is expected to be split between the two companies. A reasonable scenario would allocate $1.00 per share to Global Taste Elevation, implying a yield of 3.6% at an estimated $28 stock price, and $0.60 per share to North American Grocery, equating to a 4.2% yield at a $14 stock price. This assurance has been critical in maintaining support from income-focused investors.

A closer look at valuations highlights why management believes the breakup can unlock significant upside. Assuming Global Taste Elevation delivers $15.4 billion in sales and a 25% EBITDA margin, EBITDA would reach roughly $3.85 billion. Comparable companies in condiments and saucessuch as McCormicktrade at about 1213x EV/EBITDA. At 12x, Global Taste Elevation would be valued at around $46 billion EV; subtracting ~$10 billion of debt, equity would be about $36 billion, or $32 per share based on 1.1 billion shares outstanding.

Meanwhile, North American Grocery, with $10.4 billion in sales and a 15% EBITDA margin, would generate about $1.56 billion in EBITDA. Using an 8.5x multiple, consistent with mature packaged food peers like General Mills and Conagra, the implied enterprise value is about $13.3 billion. After accounting for ~$6 billion in net debt, the equity value comes to roughly $7.3 billion, or $6.60 per share. Adding both pieces together suggests a combined implied value of about $38.60 per share, compared to Kraft Heinz's current trading level around $27. That equates to a potential 43% upside if execution is successful and both businesses trade at peer multiples.

Valuation Scenarios: What the Spin-Off Could Be Worth

To understand how these peer multiples translate into shareholder value, we've modeled three scenarios: bull, base, and bear. These projections reflect different assumptions about what multiples the market may assign to the post-spin companies and how much debt will sit on each balance sheet.

Interpreting the Range

What emerges from this valuation framework is that the upside potential from the spin-off is highly dependent on how the North American Grocery unit is perceived and valued by the market. If investors assign it a low multiple as they often do for declining packaged food categories it could drag down the total value proposition of the entire separation.

On the other hand, if Global Taste Elevation is able to sustain premium margins and command a valuation in line with peers like McCormick, it could carry most of the weight in driving share price appreciation.

For dividend-focused investors, the combined yield (around 5.8%) remains attractive, and management's commitment to maintaining the total payout across both companies is reassuring. But for value-seeking or growth-oriented investors, the question remains whether the spin-off can catalyze a structural re-rating of Kraft Heinz's brand portfolio or simply reorganize two low-growth businesses under different tickers.

Breaking Down the Spin-Off: How Kraft Heinz's Two Future Companies Compare to Peers

In order to assess whether Kraft Heinz's 2026 spin-off could truly unlock shareholder value, investors need more than just theoretical upside projections. The key lies in benchmarking each of the two future standalone companies Global Taste Elevation Co. and North American Grocery Co. against their most relevant public comparables. This approach allows us to evaluate whether the valuation multiples being assumed in bull-case scenarios are grounded in market reality, or overly optimistic.

Peer Comparison: Metrics That Matter

Let's begin with the Global Taste Elevation segment. This business will house Kraft Heinz's more internationally oriented and faster-growing brands, such as Heinz sauces, Kraft Mac & Cheese, Philadelphia cream cheese, and Classico pasta sauces. It is expected to generate about $15.4 billion in revenue with an estimated EBITDA margin of 25%. Based on management commentary and brand positioning, it competes most directly with companies like McCormick (NYSE: MKC) and select segments of Hormel's (NYSE: HRL) international and sauces businesses.

On the other hand, the North American Grocery business will include legacy, slower-growth brands like Oscar Mayer meats, Kool-Aid, Jell-O, and Maxwell House. With $10.4 billion in sales and EBITDA margins closer to 15%, its peer set includes more mature food companies like General Mills (NYSE: GIS), Conagra Brands (NYSE: CAG), and domestic-facing parts of Hormel.

This benchmarking offers a more nuanced perspective on Kraft Heinz's valuation assumptions:

The Global Taste Elevation unit has an EBITDA margin profile that is actually superior to McCormick and Hormel's international operations. It's true that McCormick's top-line growth is slightly better, but Taste Elevation's broader brand mix and global reach could support a 12x EV/EBITDA multiple, particularly if the company continues investing in marketing and innovation.By contrast, the North American Grocery unit appears weaker. It has lower margins, slower growth, and brand categories (powdered beverages, cold cuts, processed cheese) that are not favored by health-conscious or value-seeking consumers. Peers like General Mills and Conagra both of which are better diversified and operate in more resilient categories trade at 911x EV/EBITDA. Therefore, Kraft Heinz's assumption of 8.5x for the Grocery unit may be too generous. A more realistic multiple could be closer to 6.5x7x, or even lower in a market downturn.

Next Step: Market Sentiment and Execution

While spin-offs often enjoy initial investor enthusiasm, the long-term success of this breakup hinges on execution. Global Taste Elevation will need to demonstrate growth through international expansion and innovation, while North American Grocery must show operational discipline and perhaps even M&A-driven restructuring. If execution falters, or if the market doubts Grocery's standalone relevance, the stock could remain stuck in value-trap territory.

Still, the numbers suggest a thoughtful investor can build a thesis around Kraft Heinz with a clear understanding of the risk/reward tradeoff. The spin-off isn't guaranteed to unlock value but it meaningfully changes the conversation.

Guru Moves: Buffett, Watsa, and Hedge Funds Bet on Kraft Heinz

Notably, well-known investors have sizable positions in KHC. Warren Buffett (Trades, Portfolio)'s Berkshire Hathaway remains the largest shareholder, owning about 325.6 million shares (27.5% of the company) as of June 30, 2025. Buffett (and Berkshire) has made only modest trades recently (selling ~1.5M shares at ~$53 in the past) and publicly stated the Kraft/Heinz deal was a misstep. Other value-focused investors are active: Prem Watsa (Trades, Portfolio)'s Fairfax Financial holds roughly 4.88 million KHC shares (6.4% of Fairfax's $1.84B U.S. portfolio) and increased its stake by 45% in Q2 2025. Prominent hedge funds also moved: O'Shaughnessy Asset Management (quant fund) lifted its KHC stake by +235% to 692K shares ($18M), and DE Shaw (quant) added 121% to about 1.15M shares ($30M). These transactions suggest some investors see KHC as undervalued. By contrast, corporate insiders have largely been net sellers. In Q1/Q2 2025, multiple directors (including Chair Miguel Patricio) sold shares: e.g., Patricio sold ~72K shares in Q1, and director Elio Leoni Sceti sold ~50K in early summer. These insider sales appear to be trimming or stock awards rather than dramatic de-risking, but they do signal that leadership is not aggressively buying. Overall, GuruFocus's big money players are showing mixed signals; Buffett and Fairfax hold large stakes with relatively steady exposures, whereas some hedge funds have quietly increased positions amid this reset.

Valuation Deep Dive: Forward P/E vs. 5-Year Average and Peer Comparison

KHC stock trades at a multi-year low valuation. The forward P/E is roughly 1011, compared to a historic 5-year average in the mid-teens (industry average). This depressed multiple reflects the market's low growth expectations. Correspondingly, the earnings yield (~910%) and high dividend (5.8%) make KHC attractive as an income/value play. For context, the stock's 52-week range is ~$2536, implying it's now near its low point despite cash flows being solid. On a price/book basis, KHC trades around 2.5 (reflecting a strong balance sheet and brand value), and its 5-year average PEG (forward P/E to growth) is high given weak projected growth. Compared to peers, KHC's yields and margins are above average (peers often have a 23% yield), but revenue growth is slower. Overall, valuation is below historical norms, as many investors see KHC as cheap, with value metrics suggesting the upside potential if performance stabilizes. However, analysts note that value traps are possible if fundamental headwinds persist.

Final Word: Spin-Off Could Unlock ValueBut Only if the Market Buys the Story

Kraft Heinz today stands at a strategic crossroads. For years, it has been viewed as a lumbering giant in the packaged food industryrich in brand equity, but stuck in categories increasingly out of sync with modern consumer preferences. Its share price has reflected that skepticism, languishing near decade lows, despite generating strong free cash flow and offering one of the sector's most generous dividend yields.

Now, the planned 2026 spin-off offers a rare inflection point. By breaking itself into two more focused companiesGlobal Taste Elevation Co. and North American Grocery Co.Kraft Heinz is attempting to reposition its corporate identity and unlock trapped value. The logic is clear: let the faster-growing international condiments and sauces business command a premium multiple, while the slower, cash-rich grocery unit runs independently with its own capital allocation priorities.

Our deeper peer comparison suggests this thesis has meritbut also real risk. Global Taste Elevation stacks up well against peers like McCormick and Hormel's sauces segment, with comparable margins and brand depth. Assigning it a 12x EV/EBITDA multiple is plausible, especially if the business continues to invest in innovation, expands internationally, and maintains premium pricing power. At that valuation, the Taste Elevation unit alone could be worth over $30 per Kraft Heinz share.

However, the North American Grocery unit is where the value case becomes more fragile. Even if it delivers $1.5+ billion in EBITDA, its brand portfolio includes commoditized and declining categories such as processed meats, powdered drinks, and shelf-stable cheese. The assumed 8.5x EBITDA multiple in bull cases may be too generouspeers like Conagra and Hormel's grocery segments trade closer to 79x, and in a bearish market environment, a 6x multiple is entirely possible. That could shrink its equity value to just $34 per share.

Put together, the sum-of-the-parts analysis supports a range of $2939 per share in post-spin valuation, versus Kraft Heinz's current ~$27 stock price. The upside is tangiblepotentially 3045% if peer multiples hold. But it is not guaranteed, and much of the re-rating depends on two factors: how investors value the slow-growth Grocery unit, and whether Taste Elevation can prove it truly deserves premium status in the food sector.

Importantly, this is not a classic value trap. The balance sheet is sound, the dividend appears sustainable, and cash generation remains robust. Guru investors like Warren Buffett (Trades, Portfolio) and Prem Watsa (Trades, Portfolio) still hold sizable stakes, and some hedge funds have quietly accumulated sharessuggesting institutional patience. But it's also clear that insiders are not buying heavily, and that management must still prove this spin-off isn't just cosmetic.

In short, Kraft Heinz is evolving from a one-size-fits-all story into two targeted investment cases. For value investors with a 23 year horizon and appetite for moderate risk, this may be an attractive entry pointespecially with a 5.8% yield cushioning downside. For others, the next six quarters will be a test of management credibility, market sentiment, and execution precision.

data.title: European Stocks Trim Losses Amid Hopes for US-China Trade Thaw
data.description:
European bourses pared losses on Friday, with the STOXX 50 and STOXX 600 falling 0.7% and 0.9%, after tumbling as much as 1.9% earlier in the session.

Investor sentiment improved following President Trump’s remarks that high tariffs on Chinese goods are unlikely to remain long-term, hinting at a potential easing in US-China trade tensions ahead of his upcoming meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping.

Bank shares, however, remained under pressure, led by Deutsche Bank (-5.9%), Societe Generale (-4.6%), BNP Paribas (-3.7%), Banco Santander (-3.2%), and UniCredit (-2.6%), as concerns about credit stress in US regional banks reignited fears of broader spillovers to the sector.

Rheinmetall also fell 6.5% amid China’s tighter export controls on military rare earths.

On the earnings front, EssilorLuxottica jumped more than 12% after reporting record Q3 sales, while Volvo dropped 4.8% after citing “difficult market conditions in North and South America” that weighed on its performance.

data.title: Cosmos Health Inc Regains Nasdaq Compliance

  • Stock price maintained at or above $1 for 18 consecutive days.
  • CEO credits strategic initiatives and strong fundamentals.
  • Focus remains on innovation and shareholder value.

data.title: REG - Bank of America Corp - Notice of Redemption and Cancellation of Listing
data.description:
RNS Number : 8990D Bank of America Corporation 17 October 2025  

This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014, and as the same has been retained in UK law as amended by the Market Abuse (Amendment (EU Exit) Regulations (SI 2019/310).

Notice of Redemption and Cancellation of Listing

Bank of America Corporation ("Bank of America" or the "Issuer")

The Issuer has today given notice to the holders of the €1,750,000,000 1.949% Fixed/Floating Rate Senior Notes, due October 27, 2026 (ISIN: XS2462324232; Common Code: 246232423) (the "Notes") issued under its U.S.$65,000,000,000 Euro Medium-Term Note Program that in accordance with Condition 6(c) of the Terms and Conditions of the Notes and paragraph 22 of the Final Terms dated April 22, 2022 in respect of the Notes (the "Final Terms") it will redeem all of the outstanding Notes on October 27, 2025 (the "Redemption Date"). 

The redemption price for the Notes will be equal to the Optional Redemption Amount of €1,000 per €1,000 Calculation Amount (as specified in the Final Terms) plus accrued and unpaid interest to, but excluding, the Redemption Date (the "Redemption Price").

Bank of America will request the Financial Conduct Authority (the "FCA") to cancel the listing of the Notes on the Official List of the FCA and the London Stock Exchange (the "Exchange") to cancel the admission to trading on the regulated market of the Exchange as soon as practicable after the Redemption Date.

To view the notice, paste the following URL into the address bar of your browser:

http://www.rns-pdf.londonstockexchange.com/rns/8990D_1-2025-10-17.pdf

A copy of the notice has been submitted to the National Storage Mechanism and will shortly be available for inspection at https://data.fca.org.uk/#/nsm/nationalstoragemechanism.

For further information please contact:

Lee McEntire, Bank of America, +1 980 388 6780

lee.mcentire@bofa.com

or

Jonathan G. Blum, Bank of America (Fixed Income), +1 212 449 3112

jonathan.blum@bofa.com

Forward-looking statements

Certain information contained in this notice may constitute "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. These statements are not guarantees of future results or performance and involve certain risks, uncertainties and assumptions difficult to predict or beyond our control. You should not place undue reliance on any forward-looking statement and should consider the uncertainties and risks discussed under Item 1A. "Risk Factors" in the Issuer's Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2024, and in any of the Issuer's subsequent Securities and Exchange Commission filings.  Forward-looking statements speak only as of the date they are made, and the Issuer undertakes no obligation to update any forward-looking statement to reflect the impact of circumstances or events that arise after the date the forward-looking statement was made.

DISCLAIMER - INTENDED ADDRESSEES

Please note that the information contained in the notice may be addressed to and/or targeted at persons who are residents of particular countries only and is not intended for use and should not be relied upon by any person outside these countries and/or to whom the offer contained in the notice is not addressed.  Prior to relying on the information contained in the notice, you must ascertain from the Base Prospectus whether or not you are part of the intended addressees of the information contained therein.

data.title: Hedge fund founder Leone successfully defends London harassment case
data.description:
Key points:
  • Italian hedge fund executives embroiled in London legal battle
  • Discrimination, whistleblowing claims dismissed
  • Claimant awarded reduced 1,286 pounds for unfair dismissal
  • Hedge fund secures 49,000 pounds in costs

By Nell Mackenzie and Kirstin Ridley

Hedge fund founder Davide Leone and his eponymous firm have successfully defended themselves against allegations of bullying, victimisation and discrimination in a London lawsuit brought by a former director.

However, while the Central London Employment Tribunal ruled that former senior director Jacopo Moretti had been fired after a protracted period of ill-health, it accepted his claim of unfair dismissal, partly because a disciplinary letter had been too unspecific.

He won 1,286 pounds ($1,725), sharply reduced from a basic award the judge set at 2,572 pounds because of his conduct before his dismissal.

Moretti was dismissed by Davide Leone & Partners Investment Company (DLP) in 2023 after an absence of around 13 months.

The dispute offered a rare glimpse into the often-secretive world of hedge funds, which manage more than $4 trillion.

The tribunal rejected whistleblowing and harassment claims brought by Moretti related to issues such as race, religious belief, sex and disability. Claims that he was dismissed for making health and safety disclosures and that the hedge fund failed to make reasonable adjustments for his deteriorating mental health were also rejected.

Moretti was ordered to pay just over 49,000 pounds for his share of translating 72 hours of covert Italian-language recordings of what the judge described as sometimes "emotionally charged" conversations with Leone and others that he had entered in evidence.

The sum included DLP's costs for pursuing him for non-payment of those translation and transcription fees.

In a 119-page judgment published on Tuesday, Judge Richard Nicolle said Moretti was at least partly motivated by a desire to cause "maximum embarrassment and potential reputational damage" to Leone.

The judge questioned whether all Moretti's discrimination and whistleblowing claims were made in good faith, saying in one example that Moretti had effectively "induced" Leone to speak of a colleague who he had "obvious antipathy" towards, while secretly recording him.

However, language used by Leone in the taped conversations with Moretti was "wholly inappropriate, misogynistic and discriminatory" and did not meet the standards expected of a senior executive in financial services, Nicolle said.

Leone expressed regret for his vocabulary during what he thought were private conversations, the judgment said.

Moretti's lawyer declined to comment on his client's behalf.

DLP told Reuters that as well as rejecting Moretti's claims, the judge had found an independent internal investigation at the hedge fund into Moretti's allegations to be fair and impartial.

The judgment can be appealed.

($1 = 0.7453 pounds)

data.title: Emerging Growth Research Initiates Coverage on Immuron Limited with a Buy-Extended Rating and $4.00 Price Target
data.description:
New York, New York--(Newsfile Corp. - October 17, 2025) - Emerging Growth Research today announced the initiation of coverage on Immuron Limited with a Buy-Extended rating and a 12-month price target of $4.00, representing over 80% upside from the Company's recent closing price of $2.20.

The initiation report highlights Immuron's proprietary polyclonal antibody platform, strong revenue growth from its over-the-counter product Travelan®, and an expanding clinical pipeline addressing major global infectious diseases. Near-term clinical milestones and U.S. regulatory developments add momentum to Immuron's investment outlook.

Key Highlights from the Initiation Report:

  • Innovative Technology Platform: Immuron's orally delivered polyclonal antibodies, derived from hyperimmune bovine colostrum, offer a novel, non-antibiotic approach to treating and preventing gastrointestinal infections. The technology platform has broad applicability across multiple pathogens.

  • Commercial Traction: The Company's flagship product, Travelan®, continues to post strong growth, with FY2025 revenue up 49% year-over-year to A$7.29 million. Travelan® is sold in Australia, the U.S., and Canada, and a third digestive health product, ProIBS®, is scheduled for Australian market launch in Q4 2025.

  • Clinical Pipeline: Immuron's lead drug candidates, IMM-124E (Travelan®) and IMM-529, target traveler's diarrhea and Clostridioides difficile (C. difficile) infections, respectively. Both have shown promising Phase 2 data and are advancing toward pivotal trials in 2026.

  • Strategic Partnerships: The Company collaborates with the U.S. Department of Defense, Walter Reed Army Institute of Research, and Monash University on multiple programs evaluating Immuron's antibodies for military and civilian applications.

  • Strong Balance Sheet & Global Market Potential: Immuron remains debt-free, with product revenues helping offset R&D costs. The Company addresses multi-billion-dollar global markets in antimicrobial resistance and gastrointestinal therapeutics.

According to Emerging Growth Research's analysis, while Immuron faces typical biotechnology development and funding risks, its dual-revenue model, government partnerships, and late-stage clinical catalysts position the Company for significant long-term value creation.

For a copy of the full initiation report, please visit:

https://emerginggrowth.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/Immuron-Initiation-2025-10-17.pdf

or

https://www.EmergingGrowth.com/profile/imrn/ (on the right side of the page as you scroll down)

About Immuron Limited

Founded in 1994 and headquartered in Melbourne, Australia, Immuron Limited is a commercial and clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing oral polyclonal antibodies for infectious diseases of the gastrointestinal tract. Its marketed products include Travelan® and Protectyn®, and its pipeline targets C. difficile, ETEC, and VRE infections.

For more information, please visit https://www.immuron.com.au.
